strategic_profile.executive_summary: Clemson University is a highly competitive, public land-grant research institution known for its spirited campus culture and rigorous STEM, business, and agriculture programs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Admissions have become highly competitive, dropping to a 38.3% overall acceptance rate for the Class of 2028.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must demonstrate exceptional academic rigor, and out-of-state applicants face an even tougher landscape.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median 10-year alumni earnings reach $71,513, the highest among SC public universities.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university provides exceptional return on investment, particularly for in-state residents.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Capped and closed majors (e.g., Nursing, Architecture) have significantly lower acceptance rates than general admissions.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applying with an alternate major is a critical strategy for entry into the university.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and highly spirited campus culture driven by Division I athletics (especially football), featuring a close-knit, polite student body.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Suburban setting with heavy game-day traffic and typical large-campus parking challenges.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: High-level research activity integrated with undergraduate experiential learning, particularly in manufacturing, engineering, and agriculture.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional through at least 2025. Superscores both SAT and ACT. Does not require the essay section.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate calculated based on accepted spots; overall waitlist admit rate based on offered spots was 1.01%.

gpa.notes: 92.6% of admitted students had a GPA of 3.75 or higher.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional policy in effect; submission highly recommended for capped majors.

colleges.name: College of Engineering, Computing and Applied Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for analytically driven students aiming for technical innovation, manufacturing, and software development careers.
colleges.student_archetype: Technically focused, collaborative, and engaged in experiential/co-op learning.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Students declaring CS must have a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.special_requirements: Must first complete General Engineering core courses with a C or better.
notable_professors: Dr. Alexander Leonessa (Dynamic Systems and Control)
majors.notes: Students are initially admitted to General Engineering.

colleges.name: Wilbur O. and Ann Powers College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students aiming for corporate management, accounting, or entrepreneurial ventures.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, professional, and socially engaged students.

colleges.name: College of Behavioral, Social and Health Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly competitive for healthcare tracks; requires exceptional academic standing.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers depend heavily on clinical space availability; highly competitive.
majors.notes: Some years admit zero internal transfers due to capacity constraints.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority deadline for honors college and early notification.
Also the deadline for priority scholarship consideration.

supplemental_requirements.details: Students can submit an optional personal statement to highlight specific circumstances.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Clemson heavily weighs in-state residency due to state funding mandates.

major_selection_tactics: Always list a second-choice major, particularly if applying to capped programs like Nursing, Architecture, or Engineering.
Avoid selecting the same major twice on the application.
Apply Early Action to maximize chances, as seats fill up quickly for closed majors.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to an alternate, less-impacted major can secure admission, but students should be warned that internal transfers to closed majors (like Nursing) are extremely competitive and never guaranteed.

financials.tuition_model: Differential tuition for in-state vs. out-of-state

scholarships.name: National Scholars Program
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition, fees, housing, food, and enrichment activities like study abroad.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic consideration based on honors college application.

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable annually for National Merit Scholars.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic upon reporting National Merit status.

scholarships.name: Out-of-State Tuition Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Reduces the burden of out-of-state tuition.

scholarships.name: Palmetto Fellows Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Significant tuition reduction for high-achieving SC residents.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grades of 3, 4, or 5 generally receive course credit.
exceptions: Calculus-based Physics requires a score of 5 and departmental review.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can bypass prerequisite and foundational courses.

ib_policy.general_rule: Higher Level (HL) scores of 4 or higher are awarded credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must complete 37 of their last 43 credits at Clemson.

what_it_takes: Top 10% class rank with a nearly flawless unweighted GPA.
High volume of AP/IB/Dual Enrollment courses.
Meaningful leadership and community service demonstrated in the activities list.
essay_tips: Highlight deep alignment with Clemson's values and service-oriented mission.
Address the specific challenges or unique backgrounds in the optional statement.
Showcase personality and genuine enthusiasm for the university.
red_flags: Applying to a highly impacted major without a secondary choice.
Submitting low standardized test scores when applying for closed majors.
insights: The campus has a strong small-town feel despite its massive size, creating a very tight-knit community.
Division I sports, particularly football, dominate the social scene.
In-state students have a massive advantage, and the 'Bridge to Clemson' program catches many who aren't directly admitted.
